title: Sample 6
metadata_text: Sample 6 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120315785; SRA: ERS26977772 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:13.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:11:25.920" id="59256381" accession="SAMEA120315785"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120315785</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26977772</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 6</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 with inducible SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% tetracycline free fetal bovine serum (Wisent Inc., Cat# 081150), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15699:Sample 6</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">SMARCA4</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15699:Sample 6</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:20:25.370"/> </BioSample>

title: Sample 12
metadata_text: Sample 12 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120316711; SRA: ERS26978698 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:02:17.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:11:23.366" id="59256281" accession="SAMEA120316711"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120316711</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26978698</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 12</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 p53ko, BIN-67 with SMARCA4 restoration and BIN-67 p53ko cells with SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% fetal bovine serum (Sigma, Cat# F1051), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 12</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">SMARCA4</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 12</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:20:22.023"/> </BioSample>

metadata_text: Sample 1 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120315780; SRA: ERS26977767 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:09.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:10:28.253" id="59253786" accession="SAMEA120315780"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120315780</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26977767</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 1</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 with inducible SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% tetracycline free fetal bovine serum (Wisent Inc., Cat# 081150), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15699:Sample 1</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">Control</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15699:Sample 1</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:18:58.813"/> </BioSample>

metadata_text: Sample 11 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120316710; SRA: ERS26978697 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:24.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:09:40.080" id="59251753" accession="SAMEA120316710"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120316710</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26978697</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 11</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 p53ko, BIN-67 with SMARCA4 restoration and BIN-67 p53ko cells with SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% fetal bovine serum (Sigma, Cat# F1051), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 11</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">SMARCA4</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 11</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:17:56.013"/> </BioSample>

title: MIGS Eukaryotic sample from Cryptomonas
metadata_text: MIGS Eukaryotic sample from Cryptomonas Cryptomonas BioSample: SAMN56317056; Sample name: La Parguera Cryptomonad; SRA: SRS28315490 isolate: UKO-2021-05-05 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-03-03T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-03-07T01:06:22.700" submission_date="2026-03-03T23:37:04.830" id="56317056" accession="SAMN56317056"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMN56317056</Id> <Id db_label="Sample name">La Parguera Cryptomonad</Id> <Id db="SRA">SRS28315490</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>MIGS Eukaryotic sample from Cryptomonas</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="3030" taxonomy_name="Cryptomonas"> <OrganismName>Cryptomonas</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>The cryptophyte monoculture used for sequencing was founded from a single cell isolated on 5 May 2021 from a water sample collected in the La Parguera Bioluminescent Bay, Puerto Rico. The culture was maintained in a 2 L Corning PYREX #1395 round media storage bottle plugged with a cotton stopper to permit gas exchange while excluding external microorganisms. Growth medium consisted of 1.8 L distilled water, 64.8 g Instant Ocean sea salt, 234 uL Fritz Aquatics F/2 Algae Food Part A, 234 uL Part B, and 2160 uL of a 3.33 g/L sodium selenate stock solution. Transfers were performed weekly, and 0.306 g kanamycin was added during the first two weeks to ensure an axenic culture. Genomic DNA was extracted from actively growing cells and sequenced on the Illumina MiSeq platform using paired-end sequencing. Reads were quality-filtered with fastp and normalized with bbnorm prior to assembly. The genome was assembled with SPAdes using default parameters.</Paragraph> <Paragraph>Keywords: GSC:MIxS;MIGS:6.0</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>University of Puerto Rico Mayaguez</Name> <Contacts> <Contact> <Name> <First>Brent</First> <Last>Petersen</Last> </Name> </Contact> </Contacts> </Owner> <Models> <Model>MIGS.eu</Model> <Model>MIGS/MIMS/MIMARKS.water</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="MIGS: eukaryote, water; version 6.0">MIGS.eu.water.6.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">UKO-2021-05-05</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection_date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">2021-05-05</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="depth" harmonized_name="depth" display_name="depth">0.5 meters</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="env_broad_scale" harmonized_name="env_broad_scale" display_name="broad-scale environmental context">Tropical | marine | lagoon</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="env_local_scale" harmonized_name="env_local_scale" display_name="local-scale environmental context">enbayment</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="env_medium" harmonized_name="env_medium" display_name="environmental medium">estuarine water</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geo_loc_name" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">Puerto Rico: La Parguera Bioluminescent Bay</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isol_growth_condt" harmonized_name="isol_growth_condt" display_name="isolation and growth condition">The culture was founded from a single cell isolated on 2021-05-05 from a water sample collected in the La Parguera Bioluminescent Bay, Puerto Rico. The monoculture was maintained in a 2 L Corning PYREX 1395 round media storage bottle plugged with a cotton stopper to allow gas exchange while excluding external microorganisms. The growth medium contained 1.8 L distilled water, 64.8 g Instant Ocean sea salt, 234 uL Fritz Aquatics F/2 Algae Food Part A, 234 uL Part B, and 2160 uL of a 3.33 g/L sodium selenate stock solution. Transfers were performed weekly. Kanamycin (0.306 g) was added during the first two weeks to ensure an axenic culture.</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="lat_lon" harmonized_name="lat_lon" display_name="latitude and longitude">18 N 67 W</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolation_source" harmonized_name="isolation_source" display_name="isolation source">semi-enclosed embayment</Attribute> </Attributes> <Links> <Link type="entrez" target="bioproject" label="PRJNA1431675">1431675</Link> </Links> <Status status="live" when="2026-03-03T23:37:04.830"/> </BioSample>
